title: TRIÁLOGO Opens Doors to Forgotten In-Between Spaces
content: TRIÁLOGO is a site-specific music performance in which flamenco singer Anna Murtola, electronic music artist Josu Mämmi, and a long-abandoned space engage in a three-way dialogue that reaches across time. title: Roots in the North, a heart in art – Arja Äikäs draws inspiration from nature and respect for tradition
content: Arja Äikäs is known as an active advocate of art and culture. She has participated in numerous group exhibitions and held several solo exhibitions. title: Mari Jäälinoja’s art exhibition Kirjoitettu kivi (Written Stone) at the Cultural Centre Valve
content: Cultural Centre Valve is currently presenting Mari Jäälinoja’s solo exhibition Kirjoitettu kivi (Written Stone) in its Paljetti space. In this new body of mixed-media paintings, the Oulu-based artist explores questions of identity, memory, and the profound human desire to be seen and accepted without conditions. title: Japanese Tokorozawa Fény Children’s Choir visited Oulu
content: The Japanese Tokorozawa Fény Children’s Choir visited Oulu at the beginning of May. The choir consists of 42 young singers aged between 10 and 17.
